data.title: As an implementation agent, I can consume both the design manifest and the revision image
data.description:
## Description

**What**: When a story has the design:approved tag, the sprint's task-execution prompt template includes a conditional design context section passing both the manifest path (designs/{uuid}/spatial-layout.json) and revision image path (designs/{uuid}/figma-revision.png) to the implementation sub-agent. The agent model tier is minimum sonnet (multimodal capable). The manifest governs behavioral specs; the image governs visual layout/styling; contradictions are flagged as [NEEDS_REVIEW].
**Why**: Enables the implementation agent to faithfully reproduce both the behavioral intent (from the structured manifest) and the designer's visual revisions (from the Figma export image), maximizing design fidelity without forcing multi-modal interpretation as the sole input.
**Scope**:
- IN: task-execution.md conditional design context section, model tier enforcement (sonnet minimum), manifest-authoritative-for-behavior + image-authoritative-for-visual precedence rule, [NEEDS_REVIEW] contradiction flagging
- OUT: SVG generation (S1), approval workflow (S2), sprint filtering (S3)
- SCOPE CLARIFICATION: This story is ADDITIVE to existing manifest dispatch. Non-Figma stories that have design manifests but no design:approved tag continue to receive manifest paths as before — design:approved adds the revision image path alongside, it does not gate all design context.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the sprint's task-execution.md prompt template includes a conditional section that, when a story has design:approved, passes the manifest path and revision image path to the implementation sub-agent
  ← Satisfies: "Implementation agent uses original manifest + revision image"
- When this story is complete, the implementation sub-agent is dispatched with model tier minimum sonnet (multimodal capable) when design context includes a revision image
  ← Satisfies: "implementation agent multimodal capability" from scenario analysis
- When this story is complete, the implementation sub-agent applies the precedence rule: manifest governs behavior (interactions, states, a11y, responsive rules), image governs visual layout and styling — and flags contradictions as [NEEDS_REVIEW] in its output
  ← Satisfies: "manifest governs behavior, image governs visual; contradictions flagged"
- When this story is complete, non-Figma stories with existing design manifests (but no design:approved tag) continue to receive manifest paths in task-execution dispatch — no regression to the existing non-Figma flow
  ← Satisfies: adversarial review finding on manifest-only dispatch regression
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Multimodal capability varies by model tier — test with sonnet to confirm image reading works in sub-agent context
- [Accessibility] Implementation agent must preserve ARIA specs from manifest even when revision image shows different visual layout
- [Scope] S4 is additive — design:approved adds revision image alongside existing manifest path, not gating all design context on Figma tag

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
